message:
perf(vdbe): multi-table JOIN lookup fast path, O(N) instead of Cartesian

Adds a new codegen path for SELECT/INSERT statements whose JOIN chain
can be expressed as single-row lookups against already-bound tables.
When every join in the chain is INNER or LEFT and every ON clause is
`a.col = b.col` with one side landing on a rowid / indexed column of
the new right-side table, we emit a single Rewind over the driver and
a SeekRowid (or SeekGE + IdxRowid) per dimension table instead of the
nested-loop Rewind/Next walk the existing path used.

## The problem

The existing `codegen_join_select` extended the single-table nested
scan to N tables by emitting, for each right-side table, a full
`Rewind -> Next` loop inside the outer loop.  For the 4-table FTS
rebuild query from the bd_zjisk issue #62 repro:

    INSERT INTO fts_messages(...)
      SELECT m.id, m.content, c.title, a.slug, w.path, c.source_path, m.created_at
        FROM messages m
        JOIN conversations c ON m.conversation_id = c.id
        JOIN agents a        ON c.agent_id = a.id
        LEFT JOIN workspaces w ON c.workspace_id = w.id
       ORDER BY m.rowid;

the generated program was structurally

    Rewind messages
      Rewind conversations
        Rewind agents
          Rewind workspaces
            eval ON + WHERE, emit

which is O(|messages| * |conversations| * |agents| * |workspaces|) —
372_879 * 10_083 * 2 * 105 ≈ 7.9×10^11 row combinations for the
reported repro.  Running that through the interpreter produced the
81-minute / 8-13 GB-RSS blowup described in the bug, vs 8 seconds
and ~50 MB on C SQLite which does index nested-loop joins.

## The fix

`resolve_multi_join_lookup_plan` walks the join chain once and for
each join tries to match its ON clause against any column of any
already-bound table.  The probe column's cursor is whatever cursor
holds that bound row; the lookup target is a rowid (INTEGER PRIMARY
KEY) or a single-column index on the new right table.  If every join
fits that shape, the chain gets a `MultiJoinLookupPlan`; otherwise we
fall through to the existing nested-scan path untouched.

`codegen_multi_join_lookup_select` emits the fast path:

    OpenRead driver + every right table (+ index cursors where needed)
    Rewind driver
    row_top:
      for each step i:
        evaluate probe expression from tables[0..=step.probe_table_index]
        IsNull probe -> miss
        SeekRowid right_cursor[i], probe   (or SeekGE on idx cursor)
        (on miss: INNER -> skip outer row; LEFT -> NullRow + continue)
      eval WHERE
      emit (ResultRow or SorterInsert)
      Goto row_done
      -- LEFT JOIN trailing null-row block (only for the last step)
    row_done:
      Next driver -> row_top

This is O(|driver| * log(|dim_i|)) per dimension — linear in the
driver with a handful of index seeks per row.

## Scope restrictions (conservative)

- `resolve_multi_join_lookup_plan` rejects the chain if any later
  join's probe column depends on a LEFT-joined table.  A LEFT miss
  would set the probe cursor to NullRow and downstream SeekRowid
  cannot distinguish "seek with probe = NULL" from a legitimate zero
  row, so we refuse the plan and fall back to the scan path.
- The NullRow emit block only materialises the last step's LEFT JOIN
  miss — non-terminal LEFT JOINs are rejected at plan time with a
  `debug_assert!` guarding the block so a regression on the resolver
  would panic in debug builds.
- Chains with joins on computed expressions (`SortKeySource::Expression`)
  fall through to the scan path.
- Single-table joins continue to use the specialised
  `codegen_single_join_lookup_select` (which also supports ORDER BY
  / sorters on index-equality lookups with duplicate runs).

## Regression coverage

Two new tests in `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen.rs`:

- `test_codegen_issue_62_four_table_join_uses_multi_lookup_path`:
  the exact 4-table INNER + INNER + LEFT JOIN shape from the bug.
  Asserts `Rewind` count is 1, `SeekRowid` count is 3, and the
  trailing `NullRow` stub is emitted for the LEFT workspace join.
- `test_codegen_three_table_inner_chain_uses_multi_lookup_path`:
  a 3-table INNER chain where the middle join's probe references the
  table bound by the first join (not the driver).  Asserts only 1
  Rewind, 2 SeekRowids, and no NullRow stubs.

Closes #62.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
